title: Meditations
subtitle: Τὰ εἰς ἑαυτόν (To Himself)
author: Marcus Aurelius

recommendedTranslation.title: Meditations: A New Translation
recommendedTranslation.note: Most accessible modern translation, ideal for first-time readers.

alternativeTranslations.note: Excellent scholarly translation with thorough notes and index.
More literal translation with valuable correspondence with Fronto.
Definitive scholarly edition with full commentary.

excerpt: A series of personal writings by Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius, composed in Koine Greek between 170 and 180 AD while on military campaign. Never intended for publication, these private notes record one of history's most powerful men reminding himself how to stay virtuous, humble, and rational in the face of war, plague, betrayal, and mortality. The foundational text of Stoic philosophy in practice — a masterclass in resilience, self-discipline, and living according to nature.
